items.title: Apple loses bid to pause App Store fee changes as case heads to Supreme Court
items.content: Apple lost its bid to pause court-ordered App Store payment changes, keeping external purchase links in place as its case with Epic heads toward the Supreme Court.

items.title: Paragon is not collaborating with Italian authorities probing spyware attacks, report says
items.content: Despite promising to help determine what happened with the hacks targeting journalists and activists in Italy, Israeli American spyware maker Paragon has reportedly not respon

items.title: Apple introduces a cheaper option for App Store subscriptions
items.content: Apple is adding a new subscription option that lets app developers offer lower monthly pricing in exchange for a 12-month commitment.

items.title: What Tim Cook built
items.content: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss how Apple has changed since Cook became CEO in 2011, and what challenges incoming CEO John Ternus will be facing.

items.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
items.content: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreeme

items.title: Microsoft offers buyout for up to 7% of US employees
items.content: If a worker's years of service at Microsoft plus their age equals 70 or more, they will be eligible for a voluntary retirement buyout.

items.title: Hackers are abusing unpatched Windows security flaws to hack into organizations
items.content: A security researcher published details of three security vulnerabilities in Windows Defender, and the code used to exploit them. Now, hackers are taking advantage of the vuln

items.title: Microsoft is officially killing its Outlook Lite app next month
items.content: Launched in 2022, Outlook Lite is a lightweight version of the regular Outlook app, designed for Android phones with limited storage and regions with slower internet connectio

items.title: United Airlines CEO, Rebuffed by American, Says He’s Skeptical of Other Deals
items.content: The chief executive, Scott Kirby, said that buying a smaller airline might not be worth the effort.

items.title: Warsh Clears an Early Hurdle on Path to Fed Chair
items.content: The Senate Banking Committee advanced Kevin Warsh’s nomination as Fed chair on Wednesday, teeing up the full chamber to hold a vote soon.

items.title: Uber Expands to Hotel Bookings With Expedia Partnership
items.content: In partnership with Expedia, the ride-booking, food-delivering, package-transporting service takes another step toward becoming a super app.
